name: Aventino Cucina Romana

notes: The record resolves to the same 4747 Bethesda Avenue restaurant as aventino-bethesda, whose current canonical menu has 52 formulations.
All 19 frozen rows reconcile exactly once: 15 exact, one normalized, one stale product, and two non-product artifacts.
All 19 frozen duplicate-record rows reconcile exactly once to the canonical Aventino audit: 15 exact, one normalized, one stale, and two artifacts.
The generated repository now contains only aventino-bethesda for the 4747 Bethesda Avenue restaurant.
Five frozen allergen or provenance outcomes conflict with the current owner ingredients and partial restaurant-linked affirmative labels; two rows are accurately unavailable.
The linked ordering surface is not a complete restaurant-issued allergen matrix and omitted labels are not negative evidence.
Nine frozen rows are verified, five mismatches are corrected through the canonical repair, two are accurately unavailable, and three non-current or artifact rows are not applicable.
Restaurant-issued ingredient evidence remains distinct from partial restaurant-linked affirmative labels, with no inferred negatives or cross-contact claims.
This OSM-derived record duplicates the canonical Aventino record rather than representing another location.
Its frozen output also includes a membership/navigation row and a press link as products.
The duplicate identity, same-address source graph, and all frozen row mappings were reviewed against the canonical service-aware catalog.
The duplicate record and its two non-product artifacts are absent from generated application data.

findings.summary: This OSM record is a duplicate of aventino-bethesda at the same address and on the same owner and ordering sources.
findings.resolution: Removed the OSM duplicate and retained aventino-bethesda as the only generated record.

findings.summary: One stale product and two navigation or press artifacts are represented as food rows in the duplicate record.
findings.resolution: Classified the stale row and both artifacts explicitly and excluded them from canonical generated data.

findings.summary: Five matched rows require allergen or provenance correction before the duplicate can be terminally reconciled.
findings.resolution: Mapped the five rows to the corrected canonical formulations and exact source-authority semantics.

repairs.summary: Verified duplicate removal, complete 19-row reconciliation, source-authority mapping, and canonical keeper integrity.

notes: Terra low and Sol medium both routed this record to coordinator review; the coordinator established duplicate identity and full frozen-row reconciliation.
Targeted frozen reconciliation and generated duplicate-absence regression passed.
The audit ledger retains this baseline record terminally even though it is intentionally absent from generated application data.
